source.name: Alterant by Niall Keegan
source.author: Niall Keegan
source.description:
Want to play a Transmuter in 2024 5e? Don't want to wait for WotC to release one officially? Step/fly/crawl/swim right this way!
Features of the Alterant Wizard include:
• Transmutation Savant: Even your core wizard features are transmuted! In addition to Savant's usual benefits, this one lets you take a Magic Action to transform your spellbook into any simple object of approximately the same size. • Spell Morpher: You know how sometimes you'll cast a spell that lasts 1 hour, but you only need it for 1 minute? Well, now you can transmute that spell you don't need into a Transmutation spell you do - without spending an extra spell slot. • Divided Mind: Congratulations, you've twisted your own melon! Now you can choose to take Psychic damage instead of losing Concentration on a spell. • Quick Change: If a free casting of Polymorph doesn't interest you, casting that spell as a Bonus Action probably will. • Grasp of the Alterant: No-one recycles like an Alterant. When creatures save against all effects of one of your spells, you can take a Reaction to transmute nearby matter with the wasted magic. If the creatures that saved the first time fail the DEX save against this effect, the transmuted matter grabs them and imposes the Blinded, Deafened or Restrained condition until the start of your next turn. (Serves 'em right for rolling well. Lousy punks.)
